remember: Students will be able to recall and identify the key provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000.
Students will be able to list and describe the different types of cyber crimes.
Students will be able to recall and explain the concept of cyber terrorism.
understand: Students will be able to explain the importance of the Information Technology Act, 2000, in regulating cyber crimes.
Students will be able to describe the consequences of cyber crimes on individuals and society.
Students will be able to understand the role of law enforcement agencies in preventing and investigating cyber crimes.
apply: Students will be able to apply the provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000, to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to analyze and evaluate the severity of different types of cyber crimes.
Students will be able to suggest measures to prevent and mitigate the effects of cyber crimes.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of cyber crimes on the economy and national security.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of the Information Technology Act, 2000, in preventing cyber crimes.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different laws and regulations related to cyber crimes.

ncert_objectives: To understand the key provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000.
To identify and describe different types of cyber crimes.
To analyze the impact of cyber crimes on individuals and society.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the key provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000, related to cyber crimes.
To describe the consequences of cyber crimes on individuals and society.
To suggest measures to prevent and mitigate the effects of cyber crimes.
